Home › Services › Trimming & Edging

Trimming & edging in Guelph

The details that make a lawn look finished — crisp edges, tidy trim lines, and hedges kept in shape.

Get a Free Quote Call or Text
What we trim

Edges make the lawn

A great cut with rough edges still looks unfinished. That’s why trimming around trees, fences, beds and obstacles — plus edging along walkways and driveways — is included in every Yardex mow, not sold as an add-on.

We also handle standalone work: hedge and shrub trimming, overgrown edge restoration, and one-off tidy-ups before parties, listings, or visits from your in-laws.

Get a trimming quote
Local know-how

The difference between mowed and finished

Two lawns can be cut at the same height on the same day and look completely different from the sidewalk. The difference is edges: the crisp vertical line where turf meets driveway, the clean ring around every tree and bed, hedges holding their shape instead of swallowing the front window. Trimming and edging is the finishing pass that makes a property look maintained rather than just mowed.

There's a health argument too. String-trimming around tree trunks is the number-one way homeowners girdle and slowly kill their own trees — we trim to a maintained ring instead of bare bark. And hedges cut at the right times, shaped slightly wider at the base, stay dense to the ground instead of going leggy and bare.

Every weekly mowing visit already includes basic trimming and blowdown. This service is the deeper version: re-cutting defined bed and walkway edges, shaping hedges and shrubs, and restoring lines that have grown out over a season or an ownership change.

Common questions

Frequently asked

Isn't trimming already included in mowing?

Basic trimming around obstacles and a blowdown are in every cut. This service adds the deeper work: re-cutting defined edges into beds and walkways, and shaping hedges and shrubs — the stuff a weekly visit doesn't cover.

How often do hedges need trimming?

Most Guelph hedges (cedar, boxwood, spirea) look their best with two shapings a season — early summer after the first flush, and late summer to hold the line into fall. Fast growers may want a third.

When should hedges NOT be trimmed?

During extreme heat, and hard pruning is best avoided in late fall when new growth can't harden before frost. We schedule around both — and around nesting birds in spring.

What's the difference between edging and trimming?

Trimming cuts grass the mower can't reach; edging cuts a clean vertical line where lawn meets hard surfaces or beds. Edging is what creates that sharp, finished look from the street.

Can you restore edges that have grown over?

Yes — overgrown driveway and walkway edges get re-cut back to the true line. First restoration takes longer than maintenance passes, and your quote reflects the difference honestly.

Do you clean up the clippings?

Always. Trimmings get collected and hard surfaces get blown down before we leave — the property should look better than we found it, not scattered with evidence.
